markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Store UserManual pages, sections, anchors, tool entries, feature entries, and version metadata in PostgreSQL/EventLedger. This MT belongs to UserManualAndNoContextOps. Make UserManual a built-in product surface that enables no-context model and future user operation.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The canonical stuck-together product term is UserManual; legacy ModelManual, Model Manual, and model_manual may remain only as declared deprecated shims with migration tests.
UserManual updates are build deliverables for every touched feature/tool/primitive/surface, not optional docs.
scope.expected_behavior: UserManualStorageModel leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: UserManualStorageModel is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 fcf9bce3. Migration 0310_user_manual.sql (+down): user_manual_pages (slug-keyed UserManualRecord rows, sha256 content_hash CHECK, page_kind/audience/status CHECKs, ledger_event_id FK kernel_event_ledger), user_manual_sections (typed ordered sections, UNIQUE(page_id,position)), user_manual_anchors (http_route/tauri_command/ipc_channel/cli_command/spec_anchor/test/event_type/primitive/page_link joins to real surfaces), user_manual_tool_entries, user_manual_feature_entries, user_manual_versions (seed hash + counts + receipt), user_manual_legacy_aliases. Store (src/user_manual/store.rs): race-safe ON CONFLICT(slug) page upsert with content-hash + child-count short-circuit, transactional section/anchor replace, bounded reads (LIST_CAP 500), LIKE-escaped search, KNOWLEDGE_USER_MANUAL_ENTRY_RECORDED receipts via append_kernel_event. Tests GREEN on real PG (isolated schema, full migration chain): user_manual_storage_tests 9 passed - mt194_migration_0310_creates_user_manual_tables (information_schema proof of all 7 tables), mt194_seed_is_idempotent_and_receipted (first seed = 25 page receipts + 1 corpus receipt in kernel_event_ledger; re-seed = 0 pages changed, 0 new receipts), mt194_seed_records_version_metadata (version row hash==corpus_hash, receipt id resolves in ledger), mt194_sections_ordered_and_tampered_children_heal, mt194_search_is_bounded_and_literal, plus lib store tests (content_hash deterministic+sensitive, excerpt bounded). Migration number 0310 within the assigned 0310-0319 window (wiki agent owns 0300-0309; no collision).

concerns_found_and_fixed: Tampered-children gap: hash-only short-circuit would never heal a page whose sections/anchors were deleted while the page row hash stayed intact -> upsert_page now also verifies stored child counts vs seed (deduped anchor expectation) before short-circuiting; proven by mt194_sections_ordered_and_tampered_children_heal (gut sections -> reseed restores).

Seeder race: two concurrent seeders (startup + tests / parallel lanes) could race a plain INSERT on slug -> rewritten as a single ON CONFLICT (slug) DO UPDATE ... RETURNING page_id (stable page ids, no unique-violation window).
LIKE-wildcard injection in search: % and _ in queries acted as patterns -> escaped; proven by mt194_search_is_bounded_and_literal ('%' returns fewer hits than the page count, literal semantics).
residual_risks: section_id values are regenerated on content change (replace-not-diff); cross-references use slugs + (kind,position) anchors, never section_ids, so churn is contained by design.

adversarial_review_v2.summary: Follow-up adversarial review found no blocking findings after durable ModelManual-to-UserManual wording, USER_MANUAL_VERSION HBR-MAN-001 guidance, Loom route parity, full-row freshness, receipt atomicity, and fail-closed PostgreSQL test fixes.

ready_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 working tree after fcf9bce3 includes UserManual recovery fixes; validator must review working-tree diff, not only fcf9bce3.

hardening_closed.note: State recovered to READY_FOR_VALIDATION only. completed_by remains null; validator owns PASS/COMPLETED.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-194 (UserManualStorageModel) requires storing UserManual pages, sections, anchors, tool/feature entries, and version metadata in PostgreSQL/EventLedger as durable authority. The code delivers a real 7-table migration (0310_user_manual.sql), a substantial sqlx-backed store with EventLedger receipts, and 9-11 tests proving creation, idempotent seeding, version metadata, ordered/healing children, and bounded literal search on real managed PostgreSQL.
findings: Migration src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0310_user_manual.sql:37-186 creates all 7 declared tables (user_manual_pages, _sections, _anchors, _tool_entries, _feature_entries, _versions, _legacy_aliases) with real CHECK constraints (sha256 content_hash regex, slug normalization, page_kind/audience/status/anchor_kind enums) and FK to kernel_event_ledger(event_id) created by migration 0018 (confirmed present).

store.rs:309-431 upsert_page is a real race-safe ON CONFLICT(slug) upsert with content-hash + child-row-count short-circuit (page_child_rows_match_seed, lines 433-444) so tampered/partially-deleted sections heal on reseed; transactional section/anchor replace; KNOWLEDGE_USER_MANUAL_ENTRY_RECORDED receipt appended via append_kernel_event_with_executor inside the tx (lines 332-347).
Event type wired: src/backend/handshake_core/src/kernel/mod.rs:276/350 defines KnowledgeUserManualEntryRecorded -> 'KNOWLEDGE_USER_MANUAL_ENTRY_RECORDED'. Not a fabricated string.

Real-PG proof confirmed: tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs documents 'No SQLite, in-memory, or mock fallback' and resolves to POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL/Handshake-managed ManagedPostgres; run_migrations() applies sqlx::migrate!("./migrations") (storage/postgres.rs:4355) so 0310 runs in the full chain. mt194_migration_0310_creates_user_manual_tables verifies all 7 tables via information_schema on a fresh isolated schema.

Receipt/idempotency proof is real: mt194_seed_is_idempotent_and_receipted asserts first seed = pages_total+1 receipts in kernel_event_ledger, re-seed = 0 pages changed and 0 new receipts (lines 273-328). mt194_seed_records_version_metadata resolves the version receipt id in kernel_event_ledger (lines 332-363).
search() escapes LIKE wildcards (store.rs:616-620) and is bounded by LIST_CAP/clamp; mt194_search_is_bounded_and_literal proves '%' is literal (fewer hits than page count).

Seed corpus is real content: 35 NewUserManualPage definitions + 100+ tool entries imported from the legacy ModelManual manifest (seed.rs); ensure_seeded (seed.rs:1809) drives upsert_page/upsert_tool_entry/record_version_with_receipt. Not a stub.
No deferred-live escapes: grep for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/pending MT-/*Unavailable across src/user_manual/ and the storage tests returned zero hits.

Spec alignment: anchors 10.15.8 and 2.3.13.11 resolve in current v02.194; the table set matches the UserManualRecord requirement (purpose/workflows/startup/run_commands/inputs_outputs/navigation/safety/failure_modes/recovery/hooks section_kinds at migration line 81-85). Packet cites v02.192 anchors (stale pointer) - paperwork-only, non-blocking.
Self-cert: completed_by is null; lifecycle was recovered to READY_FOR_VALIDATION leaving the COMPLETED transition to the validator (sub_rule_3 PASS).

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
